subtitle: How a merchant-saint carved a staircase of song for all of Carnatic music

panels.action: Purandara Dasa stands beside the Tungabhadra at dusk, tanpura resting against his shoulder, eyes half-closed in devotion as ruined stone gopurams and distant Hampi hills glow in golden light.

dialogue.text: Purandara Dasa of Vijayanagara, Haridasa saint and architect of the first steps of Carnatic song.

i18n.hi: विजयनगर के पुरंदर दास, हरिदास संत और कर्नाटक संगीत के पहले पायदान के शिल्पी।

panels.action: A vast aerial view of 16th-century Vijayanagara: bazaars teeming with traders, temple towers rising, the Tungabhadra winding past rocky hills, and processions of scholars and singers crossing its bridges.

dialogue.text: In the age of Krishnadevaraya, Vijayanagara thundered with trade, war and worship. Amid its stone and dust, a quieter revolution was tuning itself in song.

i18n.hi: कृष्णदेवराय के युग में, व्यापार, युद्ध और पूजा से गूंजता था विजयनगर। पत्थर और धूल के बीच, चुपचाप एक संगीत क्रांति सुर में सध रही थी।

panels.action: Inside a rich jeweller’s shop in a Karnataka town, piles of gold coins and ornaments glitter as anxious common folk stand at the doorway, hesitant and poor, facing an unseen wealthy merchant behind a low counter.

dialogue.text: Tradition remembers Srinivasa Nayaka, a wealthy jeweller, counting coins while the poor waited at his door. The Haridasa bards sang; his heart, they say, stayed silent.

i18n.hi: कहते हैं श्रीनिवास नायक, अमीर जौहरी, भीतर सिक्के गिनता, बाहर गरीब द्वार पर तरसते। हरिदास गाते रहे, उसका दिल, कहते हैं, चुप रहा।

panels.action: A middle-aged Srinivasa Nayaka, later Purandara Dasa, sits in semi-darkness on a low stool, face lit by lamplight reflected off heaps of jewellery, his expression torn between pride and an uneasy, searching doubt.

dialogue.text: Stories of his conversion bloom in legend. History is quieter: a rich man turned saint, somewhere between profit and longing, stepped onto the Haridasa path.

i18n.hi: उसके परिवर्तन की कथाएँ दंतकथाओं-सी फूल गईं। इतिहास बस इतना जानता है: लाभ और लालसा के बीच कहीं, एक धनवान ने हरिदास मार्ग पकड़ लिया।

panels.shot: wide shot by a village shrine
panels.action: Purandara Dasa, now in simple white garments with tulasi beads, stands before a small village shrine to Vitthala, hands folded, a tanpura cradled at his side as villagers look on curiously.

dialogue.text: Leaving the splendour of jewels, he took the name Purandara Dasa and placed his voice at the feet of Vittala, God of the people’s street and temple.

i18n.hi: गहनों की चमक छोड़, उसने नाम रखा पुरंदर दास और अपनी आवाज़ सौंपी विट्ठल के चरणों में, जो गली और मंदिर दोनों का देव।

panels.shot: medium group shot at a matha courtyard
panels.action: In the shaded courtyard of a Madhva matha, Purandara Dasa sits with other Haridasa singers on woven mats, Vyasatirtha’s scholarly presence hinted as a dignified elder in the background, while they discuss and sing.

dialogue.text: He moved within the Haridasa movement, shaped by Dvaita Vedanta and guided by saints like Vyasatirtha, turning philosophy into living Kannada verse.

i18n.hi: वो हरिदास आंदोलन में डूब चला, द्वैत वेदांत से ढला, व्यासतीर्थ जैसे संतों से दिशा पाकर, दर्शन को जीती-जागती कन्नड़ कविता बना गया।

panels.shot: medium shot under a village tree
panels.action: Under a spreading banyan near simple huts, Purandara Dasa sings, tanpura in hand, children and women seated before him, a farmer and a washerman listening from the edges, faces intent and moved.

dialogue.text: Unlike courtly Sanskrit, his tongue was Kannada. His devaranamas carried sly wit and deep devotion into courtyards, riverbanks and dusty lanes.

i18n.hi: दरबारी संस्कृत से अलग, उसकी ज़बान थी कन्नड़। उसके देवरनामों में छिपी नोकझोंक और गहरी भक्ति आँगनों, घाटों, धूल भरी गलियों तक पहुँची।

panels.action: A close view of Purandara Dasa’s hand plucking a string and his lips forming the word 'Purandara Vittala', as awe-struck listeners catch the phrase, some whispering it with shy reverence.

dialogue.text: Each song bore a signature, a mudra. 'Purandara Vittala,' he wove in, binding his name forever to the deity and the melody.

i18n.hi: हर गीत में होती उसकी मुहर, मुद्रा। "पुरंदर विट्ठल" बुन देता, अपना नाम सदा के लिए देव और धुन से बांध देता।

panels.action: From behind Purandara Dasa, we see several children seated in a semi-circle, trying to imitate his open-mouthed vowel as he demonstrates a simple ascending scale on a palm-leaf board scratched with svara syllables.

dialogue.text: But devotion alone was not his gift. He asked a harder question: how can anyone, from any home, learn to sing from the very first sound?

i18n.hi: पर उसकी देन सिर्फ भक्ति न थी। उसके सामने सख्त सवाल था: कोई भी, किसी भी घर से, पहला सुर गाने की शुरुआत कैसे करे?

panels.action: A top view of a palm-leaf manuscript spread before Purandara Dasa as he sits cross-legged, carefully inscribing sequences of svara syllables in straight lines while a tanpura lies beside him and a small oil lamp flickers.

dialogue.text: Patiently, he shaped svaravalis—rows of notes. Sa ri ga ma, ma ga ri sa—simple ladders, meant to steady the beginner’s first, trembling climb.

i18n.hi: धीरे-धीरे उसने रचे स्वरावली — सुरों की कतारें। सा रे ग म, म ग रे सा — आसान सीढ़ियाँ, नए गायक के काँपते कदम थामने को।

panels.action: Purandara Dasa sits amidst students in a courtyard, leading them as they clap a steady beat while singing a basic alankara pattern; his face is earnest, watching their hands and voices fall into rhythm together.

dialogue.text: From bare notes he moved to alankaras, patterns of rhythm and pitch. Not to dazzle, but to drill: making hand, breath and time walk in step.

i18n.hi: साधे स्वरों से आगे बढ़कर बनाए अलंकार, लय और सुर के खेल। चकाचौंध नहीं, अभ्यास के लिए — हाथ, सांस और समय को साथ में चलाने को।

panels.shot: gentle mid-shot with young girl learning
panels.action: A young village girl sings hesitantly as Purandara Dasa leans closer, guiding her through a simple gita, his finger tracing the melodic line in the air while other children follow softly.

dialogue.text: First, let the tune be as plain as your breath.

i18n.hi: पहले धुन को अपनी सांस जितना सरल रखो।

panels.shot: thoughtful close-up with background raga diagram faint
panels.action: Purandara Dasa’s thoughtful face fills the frame, and behind him, faintly suggested like an aura, the ascending and descending notes of a scale align in balanced symmetry.

dialogue.text: He fixed these lessons in one firm mode, the raga later known as Mayamalavagowla, giving students a single, steady ground to stand on.

i18n.hi: इन सब पाठों को उसने एक ही मज़बूत रंग पर ठोक दिया, उस राग पर जिसे बाद में मयामालवगौला कहा गया — सीखने वाले को एक स्थिर ज़मीन मिली।

panels.action: Inside a pillared stone mandapa at Hampi, Purandara Dasa leads a group of mixed disciples—Brahmins, artisans, and a shepherd—through an exercise, as temple bells hang in the background and a priest pauses to listen.

dialogue.text: In mandapas and mathas tied by Madhva thought, his method passed from voice to voice, less a secret art than a shared discipline of the body and soul.

i18n.hi: माध्व मत से जुड़ी मंडपों और मठों में, उसकी विधि आवाज़ से आवाज़ तक चली, गुप्त कला नहीं, तन और मन को गढ़ने वाला साझा अभ्यास बनकर।

panels.action: A sweeping montage shows different teachers across Karnataka years later, each in their own small space—from a modest town home to a riverbank—teaching children with similar note patterns and hand-claps, echoing Purandara’s sequence.

dialogue.text: Over decades, his graded path—svaravali, alankara, gita—spread through the Haridasa networks, carried by memory where few manuscripts travelled.

i18n.hi: सालों में उसका क्रमबद्ध रास्ता — स्वरावली, अलंकार, गीता — हरिदासों के जाल में फैल गया, यादों के सहारे वहाँ तक जहाँ पांडुलिपियाँ कम पहुँचीं।

panels.action: An older Purandara Dasa sits quietly by the Tungabhadra, his beard streaked with grey, tanpura resting silent as he gazes at children playing by the water, lips moving softly in an unvoiced composition.

dialogue.text: Tradition showers him with numbers—hundreds of thousands of songs. History, soberer, preserves only a fraction. The rest live on as absence and influence.

i18n.hi: परंपरा उस पर गिनती की बरसात करती है — लाखों भजन। इतिहास, ज्यादा संयमी, बस थोड़ा सा बचा पाया। बाकी उसकी कमी और असर बनकर जीते हैं।

panels.shot: symbolic shot of fading manuscript and living class
panels.action: In the foreground, a fragile palm-leaf manuscript lies partly faded; in the background, blurred but vibrant, a modern Carnatic class sits on the floor, a teacher guiding students through Mayamalavagowla exercises.

dialogue.text: Musicologists now sift his works by style and mudra, tracing which songs bear his true hand. Yet his real composition may be the very way musicians begin.

i18n.hi: अब संगीत-विद उसके गीतों को शैली और मुद्रा से परखते हैं, कौन सी रचना सचमुच उसकी है, धागा-धागा खोजते हैं। पर उसकी असली रचना शायद वही ढंग है, जिसमें हर गायक शुरुआत करता है।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of contemporary concert hall
panels.action: A present-day Carnatic vocalist on stage, seen from a low angle, closes her eyes as she sings a complex raga, while in a faint overlay above her, childlike notes of a basic scale rise like a ghostly staircase.

dialogue.text: Every ornate raga, every dazzling improvisation, once stood where his pupils stood—on simple steps of sound, ordered by a saint who thought first of the beginner.

i18n.hi: हर नखरीला राग, हर चमत्कारिक आलाप, कभी वहीं खड़ा था जहाँ उसके शिष्य खड़े थे — सीधे, सधे स्वरों की छोटी सीढ़ियों पर, जो एक संत ने नए सीखने वाले को सोचकर सजाईं।

panels.shot: serene medium shot by river at sunset
panels.action: Back at the Tungabhadra at sunset, Purandara Dasa sits in profile on a rock, silhouette against the glowing river, gently plucking his tanpura as birds wheel home across the sky.

dialogue.text: So Purandara Dasa endures, not in legend’s numbers, but in every first note a child learns to place, where devotion, language and discipline meet and rise together.

i18n.hi: तो पुरंदर दास आज भी ज़िंदा हैं, दंतकथा की गिनतियों में नहीं, हर उस पहले सुर में जो बच्चा सीखता है — जहाँ भक्ति, भाषा और अभ्यास साथ उठते और एक हो जाते हैं।

title_i18n.en: Purandara Dasa Sets the First Notes
title_i18n.hi: पुरंदर दास ने रचे पहली सुर-सीढ़ी

subtitle_i18n.en: How a merchant-saint carved a staircase of song for all of Carnatic music
subtitle_i18n.hi: कैसे एक व्यापारी-संत ने पूरे कर्नाटक संगीत के लिए गीतों की सीढ़ियाँ गढ़ीं
